_comment: Declarative Apify DATASET VIEWS for the Ex-Ditector evidence/capture datasets. A 'view' (transformation + display) is the post-extraction PROJECTION an actor exposes on its Console output tab and over the API (https://docs.apify.com/platform/actors/development/actor-definition/dataset-schema). Each view here carries a STIX/MISP-style distribution `marking`; sync-dataset-views.js merges the `apify_view` block into the matching actor's .actor/actor.json storages.dataset.views, and export-client.js uses `marking` to redact API exports. NO credentials, NO deployment claim. Raw locators (url/*_key/note) appear ONLY in the TLP:RED operator views.

_reference_architectures.opencti_misp_stix: Each view = a TAXII-collection-like filtered projection bound to a marking-definition; the shareable views drop raw indicators and keep only observable hashes, mirroring MISP-STIX indicator/observable fingerprinting and OpenCTI data segregation by marking.
_reference_architectures.apify_rag_browser_wcc: Each view mirrors how Website Content Crawler / RAG Web Browser publish a thin, stable, typed output projection (url,title,markdown,crawled_at) for downstream consumers instead of the raw DOM/record.

apify_view.title: Evidence (operator, full)
apify_view.description: TLP:RED — full capture rows including url + storage keys. Operator-only.

apify_view.title: Proof-of-change (shareable, redacted)
apify_view.description: TLP:GREEN — content hash + status + time only. NO url, NO storage keys. Proves a public page changed without leaking where.

apify_view.title: Compliance stops (backoff for human review)
apify_view.description: TLP:AMBER — rows where the crawler hit 401/403/429 and STOPPED instead of evading. Url/note are not shown at this marking.

apify_view.title: Evidence chain (operator, full)
apify_view.description: TLP:RED — immutable evidence-index entries with url + keys. The legal record.

apify_view.title: Change summary (shareable, redacted)
apify_view.description: TLP:GREEN — change flag + content hash + time. Closure-Mode friendly: shows THAT something changed, not a clickable destination.
